methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
OSH Cut is a bootstrapped robotics and manufacturing company founded by Caleb Chamberlain. The valuation universe spans 9 identified peers: 1 public company (277810.KQ) and 8 private firms including 1X Technologies, Foundation Robotics, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics. However, the peer set presents material constraints for traditional comparable analysis.

The peer-median method is not applicable to this valuation. The sector contains 12 robotics and manufacturing peers overall, but filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation-tier companies yields fewer than 3 datapoints in each subcategory—below the minimum threshold for reliable comparative analysis. Constructing a valuation multiple from the full sector median would introduce excessive heterogeneity in business model, maturity, and capital structure, obscuring rather than clarifying fair value.

Without sufficient narrow-peer triangulation, this analysis cannot support a specific valuation range. The primary uncertainties are OSH Cut's current revenue run rate, unit economics, customer concentration, and technical differentiation relative to the identified peer set. Any credible valuation would require either (1) expanding the dataset to capture same-stage robotics companies, (2) obtaining financial metrics from top private peers, or (3) applying alternative methods tied to founder experience, market TAM, or prototype-stage milestones. The bootstrapped status and frontier nature of the sector limit reliance on standard venture multiples.
